description: SQLAlchemy ORM models exist for all 8 core tables: documents, document_categories, extraction_schemas, extraction_fields, extracted_values, document_summaries, bulk_jobs, bulk_job_documents
steps: Import all models from src/db/models.py
Assert 8 core model classes are defined with correct table names
Assert each model has an id column of UUID type

description: Memory models defined: conversation_summaries and memory_entries tables
steps: Import ConversationSummaryModel and MemoryEntryModel from src/db/models.py
Assert both models have correct table names
Assert conversation_summaries has session_id, agent_type, summary columns

description: DocumentStatus enum includes all 7 states: uploaded, parsed, edited, classified, extracted, summarized, ingested
steps: Import DocumentStatus enum from src/db/models.py
Assert enum has exactly 7 members
Assert each expected state name is present in the enum

description: All ORM models use UUID primary keys with server-side defaults
steps: Inspect each model's id column
Assert column type is UUID
Assert server_default or default is set for UUID generation

description: All ORM models include created_at and updated_at timestamp columns
steps: Inspect each model class
Assert created_at column exists with TIMESTAMP type
Assert updated_at column exists with TIMESTAMP type and onupdate trigger

description: Foreign key relationships defined between all related tables
steps: Inspect documents model for document_category_id FK to document_categories
Inspect extraction_schemas for category_id FK to document_categories
Inspect extraction_fields for schema_id FK to extraction_schemas
Inspect extracted_values for document_id FK and field_id FK
Inspect bulk_job_documents for job_id FK and document_id FK

description: config.yml loaded with storage paths, chunking params, bulk concurrency, and RAG defaults
steps: Load config from config.yml
Assert storage.upload_dir, storage.parsed_dir, storage.schemas_dir are present
Assert chunking.max_tokens=512 and chunking.overlap_tokens=100 defaults
Assert bulk.concurrent_documents and rag.default_alpha values are present

description: .env loaded with required API keys and database URL
steps: Set OPENAI_API_KEY, REDUCTO_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL, WEAVIATE_URL, OPENAI_MODEL in environment
Load settings
Assert all values are accessible from the settings object

description: Configuration accessible as typed Pydantic settings objects
steps: Import settings class from src/config.py
Assert it inherits from pydantic BaseSettings or BaseModel
Assert type annotations are present for all config fields

description: Missing required environment variables raise clear error at startup
steps: Unset OPENAI_API_KEY from environment
Attempt to load settings
Assert a ValidationError is raised mentioning the missing variable name

description: Default values provided for non-secret config fields
steps: Load settings with minimal env vars (only secrets set)
Assert upload_dir defaults to './data/upload'
Assert max_tokens defaults to 512 and overlap_tokens defaults to 100

description: Async SQLAlchemy engine created with asyncpg driver
steps: Import engine from src/db/connection.py
Assert engine dialect name is 'postgresql'
Assert engine driver is 'asyncpg'

description: Async session factory configured with connection pooling
steps: Import session factory from src/db/connection.py
Assert pool_size=5 and max_overflow=10 on the engine
Create a session and verify it is an AsyncSession instance

description: Alembic configured and initial migration creates all tables
steps: Run alembic upgrade head against a clean database
Query information_schema.tables
Assert all 10 tables exist (8 core + 2 memory)

description: FastAPI app created with CORS middleware
steps: Import create_app from src/api/app.py
Create app instance
Assert CORSMiddleware is in the middleware stack

description: GET /api/v1/health returns 200 with healthy status when DB is reachable
steps: Start test client with database running
GET /api/v1/health
Assert status 200 and body contains {"status": "healthy"}

description: Health endpoint returns 503 when database is unreachable
steps: Start test client with database connection blocked
GET /api/v1/health
Assert status 503 and body contains {"status": "unhealthy"}

description: Lifespan handler manages database pool startup and shutdown
steps: Create app and trigger lifespan startup
Assert database engine is initialized
Trigger lifespan shutdown and assert engine is disposed

description: Document repository provides async CRUD operations
steps: Call document_repository.create() with valid document data
Call document_repository.get_by_id() and assert document returned
Call document_repository.list_all() and assert list contains the document
Call document_repository.delete() and verify document is removed

description: POST /api/v1/documents/upload accepts file and creates document with status=uploaded
steps: POST /api/v1/documents/upload with a PDF file as multipart form data
Assert response status is 201
Assert response body contains document id, file_name, status='uploaded'

description: SHA-256 dedup returns existing document for duplicate file upload
steps: Upload a file via POST /api/v1/documents/upload
Upload the same file again
Assert second response returns the same document id as the first

description: GET /api/v1/documents returns list of all documents
steps: Upload two documents
GET /api/v1/documents
Assert response contains 2 documents with id, file_name, status, file_type, file_size, created_at

description: GET /api/v1/documents/:id returns full document details
steps: Upload a document and capture the id
GET /api/v1/documents/{id}
Assert response contains all document fields including file_hash and parsed_path

description: DELETE /api/v1/documents/:id removes document and files
steps: Upload a document
DELETE /api/v1/documents/{id}
Assert response status is 204
GET /api/v1/documents/{id} returns 404

description: State machine enforces valid transitions between document states
steps: Create document with status=uploaded
Transition to parsed — assert success
Transition to classified — assert success
Transition to extracted — assert success

description: Invalid state transition returns 400 with descriptive error
steps: Create document with status=uploaded
Attempt transition to classified (skipping parsed)
Assert 400 response with message indicating required prior state

description: State transition updates status and updated_at atomically
steps: Create document and note updated_at
Perform valid state transition
Assert status is updated and updated_at is newer than before

description: get_available_actions returns valid next transitions for current status
steps: Create document with status=parsed
Call get_available_actions()
Assert returned list contains 'classify' and 'edit'

description: Reducto client parses document and returns markdown
steps: Send a test PDF to Reducto client
Assert returned content is non-empty markdown string
Assert markdown preserves table structures

description: Parsed markdown saved to filesystem and document record updated
steps: Parse a document via Reducto client
Assert file exists at parsed_dir/{document_id}.md
Assert document.parsed_path is updated in database

description: POST /api/v1/parse/:id triggers parsing and transitions status to parsed
steps: Upload a document
POST /api/v1/parse/{id}
Assert response contains parsed markdown content
GET /api/v1/documents/{id} and assert status is 'parsed'

description: GET /api/v1/parse/:id/content returns parsed markdown
steps: Parse a document
GET /api/v1/parse/{id}/content
Assert response status 200 with non-empty markdown content

description: PUT /api/v1/parse/:id/content saves edited content and transitions to edited
steps: Parse a document
PUT /api/v1/parse/{id}/content with modified markdown
Assert response status 200
GET document and assert status is 'edited'

description: Parsing skipped for unchanged file hash with existing parsed content
steps: Parse a document
POST /api/v1/parse/{id} again without changing the file
Assert response returns existing parsed content with 200 (no Reducto call made)

description: DeepAgent orchestrator created with openai:gpt-5.4-mini model
steps: Import orchestrator from agent factory
Assert agent is initialized with model 'openai:gpt-5.4-mini'
Assert agent instance is not None

description: Subagent registry has 5 named slots: classifier, extractor, judge, summarizer, rag_retriever
steps: Get orchestrator subagent registry
Assert 5 subagents are registered
Assert names match: classifier, extractor, judge, summarizer, rag_retriever

description: Middleware stack includes FilesystemMiddleware, SubAgentMiddleware, SummarizationMiddleware
steps: Inspect orchestrator middleware configuration
Assert FilesystemMiddleware is present
Assert SubAgentMiddleware is present
Assert SummarizationMiddleware is present

description: Agent factory provides thread-safe singleton access
steps: Call get_orchestrator() twice concurrently
Assert both calls return the same instance
Assert async lock is used for initialization

description: Orchestrator initializes and responds to a basic prompt
steps: Initialize orchestrator
Send a basic health-check prompt
Assert a non-empty response is returned without errors

description: PII middleware registered as DeepAgents pre-model callback
steps: Inspect orchestrator middleware stack
Assert PII middleware is registered as pre-model callback
Assert it executes before LLM calls

description: PII middleware detects and redacts SSN, phone, email, bank accounts, addresses
steps: Pass content containing SSN '123-45-6789', phone '(555) 123-4567', email 'john@example.com'
Assert each is replaced with typed placeholder: [REDACTED_SSN], [REDACTED_PHONE], [REDACTED_EMAIL]
Pass content with bank account and address, assert [REDACTED_ACCOUNT] and [REDACTED_ADDRESS]

description: Fund names and financial terms pass through PII middleware unredacted
steps: Pass content containing 'Horizon Equity Partners IV', 'management fee of 2%', 'carried interest of 20%'
Assert all financial terms remain unchanged in output
Assert 'preferred return' and 'fund term' pass through unredacted

description: Original unredacted content is never sent to LLM
steps: Configure PII middleware on agent
Send content with PII through agent
Intercept LLM call and assert the content passed contains [REDACTED_*] placeholders, not original PII

description: ShortTermMemory provides per-session message storage with configurable max
steps: Create ShortTermMemory(max_messages=20)
Add 5 messages to session 'test-1'
Assert get_messages('test-1') returns 5 messages
Assert get_messages('test-2') returns empty list

description: Messages automatically trimmed when exceeding max_messages
steps: Create ShortTermMemory(max_messages=5)
Add a system message and 6 user/ai messages
Assert total messages is 5
Assert system message is preserved as first message

description: Session operations: add messages, clear, delete, count
steps: Add messages to two sessions
Assert get_session_count() returns 2
Call clear_session('s1') and assert messages are empty but session exists
Call delete_session('s1') and assert get_session_count() returns 1

description: get_conversation_summary returns last 6 messages formatted as text
steps: Add 10 messages to a session
Call get_conversation_summary(session_id)
Assert returned string contains content from the last 6 messages

description: Alembic migration creates conversation_summaries and memory_entries tables
steps: Run alembic upgrade head
Query information_schema.tables for conversation_summaries
Query information_schema.tables for memory_entries
Assert both tables exist

description: save and get conversation summary with upsert semantics
steps: Call save_conversation_summary() with test data
Call get_conversation_summary(session_id) and assert data matches
Call save_conversation_summary() again with updated summary
Assert get returns the updated summary (upsert, not duplicate)

description: Generic key-value store: put, get, delete, search operations
steps: Call put('ns1', 'key1', {'value': 'test'})
Call get('ns1', 'key1') and assert data matches
Call search('ns1') and assert result contains the entry
Call delete('ns1', 'key1') and assert get returns None

description: Long-term memory operations use retry logic for transient errors
steps: Mock database to raise a transient OperationalError on first call
Call save_conversation_summary()
Assert operation succeeds after retry (no exception raised)

description: GET /api/v1/config/categories returns all categories
steps: Seed two categories in database
GET /api/v1/config/categories
Assert response contains 2 categories with name, description, classification_criteria

description: POST /api/v1/config/categories creates a new category
steps: POST /api/v1/config/categories with name='LPA', description, classification_criteria
Assert response status 201 with created category including id
GET /api/v1/config/categories and assert new category is in list

description: PUT /api/v1/config/categories/:id updates category
steps: Create a category
PUT /api/v1/config/categories/{id} with updated name
Assert response contains updated name

description: DELETE /api/v1/config/categories/:id returns 400 if documents assigned
steps: Create category and assign a document to it
DELETE /api/v1/config/categories/{id}
Assert response status 400 with message about assigned documents

description: GET /api/v1/config/categories/:id/fields returns extraction fields ordered by sort_order
steps: Create category with 3 extraction fields at sort_order 1, 2, 3
GET /api/v1/config/categories/{id}/fields
Assert fields returned in sort_order sequence

description: POST /api/v1/config/categories/:id/fields creates extraction fields
steps: Create a category
POST /api/v1/config/categories/{id}/fields with list of field definitions
Assert fields are created with field_name, display_name, data_type, required, sort_order

description: Default Other/Unclassified category seeded on startup
steps: Start application with empty database
GET /api/v1/config/categories
Assert at least one category exists with name 'Other' or 'Unclassified'

description: Classifier subagent returns ClassificationResult with category and reasoning
steps: Provide parsed LPA document content and categories to classifier
Assert response is ClassificationResult with category_id (UUID), category_name (str), reasoning (str)
Assert category_id matches an existing category in database

description: Classifier has get_categories and get_parsed_content tools
steps: Inspect classifier subagent tools
Assert get_categories tool is registered
Assert get_parsed_content tool is registered

description: Classifier returns Other/Unclassified when no category matches
steps: Provide a document that does not match any defined categories
Assert classifier returns the 'Other/Unclassified' category_id
Assert reasoning explains why no category matched

description: POST /api/v1/classify/:id triggers classification and transitions to classified
steps: Upload and parse a document
POST /api/v1/classify/{id}
Assert response contains category_id, category_name, reasoning
GET document and assert status is 'classified' and document_category_id is set

description: Classification API returns 400 if document is not parsed or edited
steps: Upload a document (status=uploaded, not yet parsed)
POST /api/v1/classify/{id}
Assert response status 400 with message about required status

description: Extractor builds dynamic Pydantic model from extraction fields
steps: Create category with fields: fund_name (string), management_fee (percentage), fund_term (number)
Invoke extractor for a classified document
Assert dynamic Pydantic model has matching attributes with correct types

description: Extractor returns ExtractionResult with field_name, extracted_value, source_text per field
steps: Run extractor on a parsed LPA document with defined extraction schema
Assert response contains list of ExtractedField objects
Assert each field has non-empty field_name, extracted_value, and source_text

description: Extractor uses DeepAgent response_format for structured output
steps: Inspect extractor subagent configuration
Assert response_format is set to the dynamic Pydantic model
Assert structured_response key is used in the agent response

description: Judge returns confidence rating (high/medium/low) with reasoning per field
steps: Provide extracted values with source texts to judge subagent
Assert response is JudgeResult with list of FieldEvaluation
Assert each evaluation has field_name, confidence in ['high','medium','low'], and non-empty reasoning

description: Judge is a separate subagent independent from extractor
steps: Inspect agent registry
Assert judge and extractor are distinct subagent instances
Assert judge makes a separate LLM call (not shared with extractor)

description: Judge confidence criteria: high=explicit, medium=implied, low=vague/contradictory
steps: Provide extraction with clear source text — assert high confidence
Provide extraction with interpretive source — assert medium confidence
Provide extraction with vague/contradictory source — assert low confidence

description: POST /api/v1/extract/:id runs extractor then judge and saves results
steps: Upload, parse, classify a document
POST /api/v1/extract/{id}
Assert response contains extraction results with confidence scores
Query extracted_values table and assert results are persisted

description: GET /api/v1/extract/:id/results returns full extraction results
steps: Extract a document
GET /api/v1/extract/{id}/results
Assert response contains list with field_name, display_name, extracted_value, source_text, confidence, requires_review, reviewed

description: PUT /api/v1/extract/:id/results updates values and marks as reviewed
steps: Extract a document with low-confidence fields
PUT /api/v1/extract/{id}/results with updated value for a field
GET results and assert the field has reviewed=true and updated value

description: Hard review gate blocks status transition if unreviewed low-confidence fields exist
steps: Extract a document producing low-confidence fields
Attempt to transition status to extracted without reviewing
Assert 400 response with list of unreviewed field names

description: Status transitions to extracted after all low-confidence fields reviewed
steps: Extract document, review all low-confidence fields via PUT
Transition status to extracted
Assert success and document status is 'extracted'

description: Summarizer subagent returns SummaryResult with summary and key_topics
steps: Provide parsed document content to summarizer
Assert response is SummaryResult with non-empty summary (str) and key_topics (list[str])

description: POST /api/v1/summarize/:id generates summary and transitions to summarized
steps: Prepare an extracted document
POST /api/v1/summarize/{id}
Assert response contains summary text and key_topics
GET document and assert status is 'summarized'

description: GET /api/v1/summarize/:id returns existing summary
steps: Summarize a document
GET /api/v1/summarize/{id}
Assert response contains summary and key_topics matching what was generated

description: Summarizer regenerates when content hash differs
steps: Summarize a document
Edit the parsed content (changing content hash)
POST /api/v1/summarize/{id} again
Assert a new summary is generated (not cached)

description: Weaviate client connects and DocumentChunks collection created
steps: Initialize Weaviate client with WEAVIATE_URL
Assert client is connected
Assert DocumentChunks collection exists with text2vec-openai vectorizer

description: SemanticChunker splits markdown with fallback to token-based chunking
steps: Chunk a long markdown document using SemanticChunker
Assert output is a list of chunks, each <= 512 tokens
Assert chunks have proper overlap (~100 tokens)

description: POST /api/v1/ingest/:id chunks and upserts to Weaviate with metadata
steps: Prepare a summarized document
POST /api/v1/ingest/{id}
Query Weaviate for chunks with document_id filter
Assert chunks exist with metadata: document_id, document_name, document_category, chunk_index

description: Re-ingestion deletes existing chunks before inserting new ones
steps: Ingest a document
Count chunks in Weaviate for that document_id
Re-ingest the same document
Assert chunk count is the same (old deleted, new inserted)

description: Document status transitions to ingested after successful ingestion
steps: Ingest a document via POST /api/v1/ingest/{id}
GET /api/v1/documents/{id}
Assert status is 'ingested'

description: RAG retriever performs hybrid search with configurable alpha
steps: Ingest a document into Weaviate
Query with alpha=0.0 (pure keyword) and assert results returned
Query with alpha=1.0 (pure semantic) and assert results returned
Query with alpha=0.5 (hybrid default) and assert results returned

description: POST /api/v1/rag/query returns answer with citations
steps: Ingest a document
POST /api/v1/rag/query with query='What is the fund name?', scope='single_document', scope_id={doc_id}
Assert response contains answer (str) and citations (list with chunk_text, document_name, relevance_score)

description: Scope filters applied correctly: single doc, all, by category
steps: Ingest two documents in different categories
Query with scope='single_document' — assert only matching doc chunks returned
Query with scope='all' — assert chunks from both documents
Query with scope='by_category' — assert only matching category chunks

description: React + Vite + TypeScript project with Tailwind CSS scaffolded in frontend/
steps: Check frontend/package.json exists with react, vite, typescript, tailwindcss dependencies
Run npm run build and assert it succeeds
Assert tailwind.config.ts exists

description: React Router configured with all 10 application routes
steps: Inspect router configuration

Assert routes exist for: /, /upload, /documents/:id/parse, /documents/:id/classify, /documents/:id/extract, /documents/:id/summary, /documents/:id/chat, /config/categories, /config/extraction-fields, /bulk

description: Axios API client with baseURL and case transformers configured
steps: Import apiClient from frontend lib
Assert baseURL is http://localhost:8000/api/v1
Assert request interceptor converts camelCase to snake_case
Assert response interceptor converts snake_case to camelCase

description: TanStack Query provider with staleTime and retry config
steps: Inspect QueryClient configuration
Assert staleTime is 300000 (5 minutes)
Assert retry does not retry on 404, 401, 403 status codes

description: Design token system in tailwind.config.ts with color palettes and fonts
steps: Read tailwind.config.ts
Assert primary, success, warning, error color palettes are defined
Assert fontFamily includes Inter and Poppins

description: Dashboard at / renders document table with status badges
steps: Navigate to /
Assert table renders with columns: file_name, status, category, file_type, file_size, created_at
Assert DocumentStatusBadge renders with correct colors per status

description: Document rows link to next action based on status
steps: Render dashboard with document in status=uploaded
Assert row links to /documents/:id/parse
Render with status=classified, assert row links to /documents/:id/extract

description: Empty state shown with link to upload when no documents exist
steps: Render dashboard with empty document list
Assert empty state message is displayed
Assert link to /upload is present

description: Upload page renders drag-drop zone accepting PDF, DOCX, XLSX, images
steps: Navigate to /upload
Assert drag-drop zone is rendered
Drop a PDF file and assert it is accepted
Drop a .exe file and assert it is rejected

description: Upload shows progress and navigates to parse page on success
steps: Upload a file via the upload page
Assert progress indicator is shown during upload
Assert navigation to /documents/:id/parse after successful upload

description: Duplicate file upload shows notification with link to existing document
steps: Upload a file that already exists (API returns existing document)
Assert notification displayed: 'File already exists'
Assert link to existing document is shown

description: Parse page renders split view with document info and TipTap editor
steps: Navigate to /documents/:id/parse for a parsed document
Assert left panel shows document info (filename, status, size)
Assert right panel renders TipTap editor with parsed markdown content

description: Parse and Save buttons trigger correct API calls
steps: Click 'Parse Document' button and assert POST /api/v1/parse/:id is called with loading spinner
Edit content in TipTap editor
Click 'Save Edits' and assert PUT /api/v1/parse/:id/content is called

description: Proceed to Classify button visible for parsed/edited documents
steps: Render parse page for document with status=parsed
Assert 'Proceed to Classify' button is visible
Click button and assert navigation to /documents/:id/classify

description: Category manager at /config/categories displays categories with CRUD actions
steps: Navigate to /config/categories
Assert categories displayed as cards with name, description, edit/delete buttons
Click 'Add Category' and assert modal opens with name, description, criteria fields

description: Extraction field editor shows fields grouped by category with drag-drop reorder
steps: Navigate to /config/extraction-fields
Assert fields displayed grouped by category
Drag a field to reorder and assert sort_order updates

description: Field form includes all required inputs with correct types
steps: Open add field form
Assert inputs: field_name, display_name, description, examples (textarea), data_type (dropdown), required (checkbox)
Assert data_type dropdown options: string, number, date, currency, percentage

description: Classification page displays detected category with reasoning
steps: Navigate to /documents/:id/classify for a parsed document
Click 'Classify' button
Assert detected category name and reasoning text are displayed

description: Category override dropdown allows manual category selection
steps: On classification page, open category override dropdown
Assert all categories are listed
Select a different category and assert it updates

description: Accept & Proceed saves classification and navigates to extract page
steps: Classify a document
Click 'Accept & Proceed'
Assert navigation to /documents/:id/extract

description: Extraction page renders 3-column table with confidence badges
steps: Navigate to /documents/:id/extract for an extracted document
Assert 3-column table: Field Name, Extracted Value, Source Text
Assert confidence badges: green (high), yellow (medium), red (low)

description: Low-confidence rows highlighted with Requires Review label and inline edit
steps: Render extraction page with low-confidence fields
Assert low-confidence rows have amber background and 'Requires Review' label
Click 'Edit' and assert inline input appears for the field value

description: Save & Proceed button disabled until all flagged fields are reviewed
steps: Render extraction page with unreviewed low-confidence fields
Assert 'Save & Proceed' button is disabled with message 'Review all flagged fields first'
Review all flagged fields
Assert button becomes enabled

description: Summary page displays summary text and key topics as tags
steps: Navigate to /documents/:id/summary for a summarized document
Assert summary text is displayed
Assert key topics rendered as styled tag chips

description: Generate and Regenerate buttons trigger summarization API
steps: Click 'Generate Summary' and assert POST /api/v1/summarize/:id called with loading state
Assert summary appears after generation
Click 'Regenerate' and assert new summary replaces old

description: Proceed to Ingest & Chat button triggers ingestion and navigates to chat
steps: Click 'Proceed to Ingest & Chat' on summary page
Assert POST /api/v1/ingest/:id is called
Assert navigation to /documents/:id/chat

description: Chat page renders message interface with scope and search mode controls
steps: Navigate to /documents/:id/chat
Assert message input field is present
Assert scope selector with options: This Document, All Documents, By Category
Assert search mode toggle: Semantic, Keyword, Hybrid

description: Query submission renders AI response with expandable citation cards
steps: Submit a query in the chat input
Assert AI response message appears with answer text
Assert citation cards are rendered with chunk_text, document_name, relevance_score
Click a citation card and assert it expands to show full chunk text

description: Chat history maintained in session for multi-turn display
steps: Submit two queries sequentially
Assert both user messages and AI responses are displayed in order
Assert conversation thread is maintained visually

description: LangGraph StateGraph with 7 pipeline nodes
steps: Inspect the bulk StateGraph definition
Assert 7 nodes: parse_node, classify_node, extract_node, judge_node, summarize_node, ingest_node, finalize_node
Assert nodes are connected in sequence

description: DocumentState TypedDict carries document through pipeline
steps: Inspect DocumentState definition
Assert fields: document_id, status, parsed_content, classification_result, extraction_results, summary, error, timing metrics

description: Per-document error handling: failures don't block other documents
steps: Submit 3 documents where document 2 will fail at classify_node
Run pipeline
Assert documents 1 and 3 complete successfully
Assert document 2 has error captured in DocumentState.error

description: Concurrent processing of up to 10 documents per run
steps: Submit 10 documents to the bulk pipeline
Assert all 10 are processed concurrently (not sequentially)
Assert total time is significantly less than 10x single-document time

description: MemorySaver checkpointing enabled for pipeline resumability
steps: Inspect StateGraph compile configuration
Assert MemorySaver is configured as the checkpointer

description: POST /api/v1/bulk/upload creates bulk job and starts processing
steps: POST /api/v1/bulk/upload with 3 files
Assert response contains job_id with status=pending
Assert 3 bulk_job_documents records created

description: GET /api/v1/bulk/jobs returns list with status summary
steps: Create a bulk job
GET /api/v1/bulk/jobs
Assert response contains job with id, status, total_documents, processed_count, failed_count

description: GET /api/v1/bulk/jobs/:id returns per-document breakdown
steps: Create and process a bulk job
GET /api/v1/bulk/jobs/{id}
Assert response includes per-document details: document_id, file_name, status, error_message, processing_time_ms

description: Failed documents have error_message populated
steps: Submit a bulk job with a corrupted file that fails parsing
GET /api/v1/bulk/jobs/{id}
Assert failed document has status='failed' and non-empty error_message

description: Bulk dashboard at /bulk displays jobs with progress bars
steps: Navigate to /bulk
Assert bulk jobs listed with status badge and progress bar (processed/total)
Assert timestamps shown for each job

description: Multi-file drag-drop upload zone for new bulk jobs
steps: Navigate to /bulk
Assert 'New Bulk Job' section with drag-drop zone
Drop multiple files and assert they are queued for upload

description: Per-document progress view with status indicators and error details
steps: Expand a bulk job row
Assert each document shows status indicator (pending=gray, processing=blue, completed=green, failed=red)
Assert failed documents display error_message text

description: Job list auto-refreshes with 5-second polling while processing
steps: Create a bulk job (status=processing)
Assert TanStack Query refetchInterval is 5000ms
Wait for job to complete
Assert polling stops when no jobs have status=processing
